---
title: "HW 04: One-Way ANOVA"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Computational |
| **Due** | Week 5 |
| **Weight** | Part of Homework (50% of final grade) |
| **Grading** | Completion-based |
| **Dataset** | `pisa2000` (USA, UK, Germany) from [`regdatasets`]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"} |
| **Related Reading** | [Ch 03: One-Way ANOVA]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/03-one-way-anova.html){target="_blank"} |
| **Related Lab** | [Lab 04: Categorical Contrasts]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}/labs/lab04-categorical-contrasts.html){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-note}
## Grading Policy
Homework is graded on **completion**. Submit a good-faith effort for full credit. A good-faith effort means: (1) all problems are attempted, (2) for computational assignments, R code runs without errors, and (3) written interpretations are included where requested.
:::

## Setup

This assignment uses the `pisa2000` dataset from the `regdatasets` package. You will analyze data from **three countries**: the United States, the United Kingdom, and Germany. The outcome variable is `wleread` (reading literacy score) and the predictor is country.

## Problems

### Problem 1

Choose one country as the **reference category**. Create dummy variables for the remaining two countries. Regress `wleread` on the dummy variables and report the regression output. Interpret each coefficient (intercept and slopes) in context.

### Problem 2

a. Conduct an ANOVA *F*-test of the null hypothesis that mean reading scores are equal across the three countries. Use the **5% significance level**. Report the *F*-statistic, degrees of freedom, and $p$-value, and state your conclusion.

b. Where can you find the *F*-test result in the regression output from Problem 1? Explain.

c. Conduct **pairwise comparisons** among the three countries using the **Sidak** correction for multiple testing. Which pairs of countries have significantly different mean reading scores?


## Submission

1. Download the starter `.qmd` file and data from Blackboard.
2. Complete the analysis in R, filling in the code chunks and written responses.
3. Render your Quarto document to **PDF** and submit to Blackboard by the due date.
